name: plado

metadata.name: plado
metadata.summary: Python utils for lifted PDDL planning, including PDDL parser and lifted successor generation.

documentation:
*Coming soon.*

In the meantime, you can take a look at the sample uses in the `examples/` directory, which show all the core functionality.

Requirements: Python >= 3.10

Name is pronounced "PLAY-doh", it's catchy, playful, and easy to say - according to ChatGPT.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Atom.standardize_arguments
documentation:
After standardization, every argument is a unique variable. Might create
fresh variables to satisfy this property. Calls notify_variable for
variables newly introduced substituing duplicate occurences of the same
variable, and notify_object for variables substituing objects, in both
cases passing the old argument as first and the new variable as second argument.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Atom.substitute
documentation:
Substitutes all variables according to the given substitution
mapping.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Atom.substitute_
documentation:
Substitutes all variables according to the given substitution
mapping.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Clause.is_trivial
documentation: A clause is trivial if its body is empty.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Clause._make_0_indexed
documentation: Replaces variable ids, making them range from [0, ..., num_variables)

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Clause._merge_variables
documentation:
Finds each equality atom in the body containing two
variables. Replaces the two variables by a unique representative.

methods.name: plado.datalog.program.Clause.standardize_variables
documentation:
Makes sure all atoms in the body have only variables as arguments,
and that no variable appears twice in any atom. Introduces additional
variables and equality atoms to satisfy these properties.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks._check_unique_names

params.name:
pddl.Argument
        | pddl.ArgumentDefinition
        | pddl.Type
        | pddl.Predicate
        | pddl.Function
        | pddl.Action

documentation: Checks that all argument names are unique.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_object_names
documentation: Checks that all object have a unique name.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_type_names
documentation: Checks that all types have a unique name.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_action_names
documentation: Checks that all actions have a unique name.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_predicates
documentation: Checks that all predicates have a unique name.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_functions
documentation: Checks that all functions have a unique name.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.no_reserved_functions
documentation:
Checks that no reserved function name is used (i.e.,
total-cost, reward, and goal-reward). If they are defined, they are
expected to have 0-arity.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.unique_variable_names
documentation:
Checks that variable names are unique at every place where variables might appear,
i.e.,  in predicate and function definitions, in action schema parameters, in
existential and universal quantification, and universal effects.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.type_hierarchy
documentation:
Checks that all referenced type names in the type hierarchy are defined
and that the induced type inheritance graph is acyclic.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.predicate_references
documentation:
Checks that all atoms reference a defined predicate with matching arity, and that
all function calls reference a defined function with matching arity.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.check_domain_variable_constant_references
documentation:
Check that all atoms and function calls in the domain reference defined arguments
(constants and variables).

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.check_problem_variable_object_references
documentation:
Checks that all atoms and function calls in the problem reference existing
objects or variables.

functions.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.make_checks
documentation:
Performs syntactic sanity checks (type, function, predicate, variable, object, and
action names and references).

classes.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.ExpressionArgumentChecker
documentation: Checks that the arguments of every function call is defined.

classes.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.ConditionArgumentChecker
documentation:
Checks that the arguments of every atom and every function call
appearing in some numeric condition is defined.

classes.name: plado.parser.sanity_checks.EffectArgumentChecker
documentation: Checks that all atoms and function calls have defined arguments.

functions.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.filtering.insert_filter_predicates
documentation: Insert all = and != predicates into the join tree.

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.__iter__
documentation: Returns self reference.

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.__next__
documentation: Returns and consumes next token from the stream.

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.__getitem__
documentation: @see at()

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.at
documentation:
Lookahead the i-th next token (i=0 is the next token, and so on).
Does change the external state of the iterator, i.e., calling
at(i) multiple times will return the exact same token.

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.shift
documentation: Returns and consumes the next n tokens from the stream.

methods.name: plado.parser.parser.LookaheadStreamer.lookahead
documentation: Returns the next n tokens from the stream without consuming them.

classes.name: plado.datalog.numeric.NumericConstraint

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._select
documentation:
Greedily select two nodes in the current list to join. Evaluate cost
function for all pairs, and return the cheapest option.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._select_for_join
documentation:
Greedily try out all possible combinations of two nodes in the current
list with interesecting arguments (hence join). Evaluate cost function
for all pairs, and return the cheapest option.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._select_for_product
documentation:
Similar to _select_for_join; but here try out *all* possible combinations of
nodes. This is necessary, if the sub-graph spanned by nodes is not
connected. The considered join sub-graph might get disconnected as part
of removing negative nodes.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._join_nodes
documentation:
Join the two nodes and apply any subtraction whose arguments are contained
in the join result.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._apply_subtractions
documentation:
Immediately apply all ``set minus joins'' (subtractions) for the
relations in the subtraction list whose parameters are fully contained
in node.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._select_and_join
documentation: Select two nodes from the node list and replace them by their join.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._create_jt_nodes
documentation:
Create the QNodes for the join-graph nodes whose ids are contained in
node_ids. Negative nodes are handled and returned separately. This function
returns a pair, whose first element is the list of all QNodes for the
referenced non-negative join nodes. The second element is a mapping from
the relations' arguments to a list of QNodes which must be subtracted
from the joins.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._compute_join_ordering
documentation: Serialize the given join-graph nodes into a tree.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer._compute_components
documentation:
Decompose join graph into connected components. Each connected component
is joined individually. Performing the cross product of all joins
afterwards.

methods.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.planner.GreedyOptimizer.__call__
documentation: Serialize the given join graph into a tree.

functions.name: plado.utils.search.lower_bound
documentation:
Returns the first element in the range [left, right[ in values
that does not compare less to value, or right if no such value exists.

functions.name: plado.utils.search.upper_bound
documentation:
Returns the first element in the range [left, right[ in values
where value compares less to, or right if no such value exists.

functions.name: plado.utils.search.search
documentation:
Search in random-access container values for value in the range defined by
[left, right[ (i.e., excluding right). Returns index of the element
comparing equal to value, or None if no such element exists.

functions.name: plado.utils.graph.tarjan
documentation:
Run Tarjan's algorithm on the given graph. Notifies on_scc whenever a
max-SCC is found. stack is the recursive node expansion stack (optional).
get_successor takes a node (of type T) as input and returns its neighbors
in the graph. init is the root node from where search is started.

functions.name: plado.utils.graph.reachability_closure
documentation:
Computes the transitive reachability closure of all nodes reachable from
init. Similarly to tarjan, get_successors returns a node's neighbors
in the graph.

classes.name: plado.utils.union_find.UnionFind
documentation: Simple union find data structure.

classes.name: plado.datalog.evaluator.compiler.ExecutionTreeBuilder
documentation:
Traverses the join tree, collecting information needed for
building up index structures to implement the joins.
